sections.title: Market Pulse: Tech Sell-off and Oil Concerns Dominate Weekend Outlook

items.headline: Markets Closed Sunday, Futures Point to Lingering Caution After Friday's Declines
items.summary: U.S. equity markets are closed today, Sunday, July 19, 2026. However, market sentiment heading into the new week remains cautious, largely influenced by Friday's widespread sell-off in technology stocks and persistent concerns over escalating geopolitical tensions in the Middle East driving oil prices higher. Futures markets, which will reopen for Sunday evening trading, are expected to reflect this subdued sentiment. The S&P 500 recorded its first losing week in three, with the tech-heavy Nasdaq Composite experiencing significant declines.

items.statContext: Fell 1.0% Friday, ending its first losing week in three.
Dropped on Friday, pressured by broader market weakness.
Sank significantly as AI and tech stocks sold off.

items.headline: Netflix Plunges on Weak Revenue Forecast
items.summary: Shares of Netflix ($NFLX) fell 7.3% on Friday, following an 11% pre-market drop, after its second-quarter revenue guidance missed analysts' expectations.

items.headline: Intuitive Surgical Drops Despite Earnings Beat
items.summary: Intuitive Surgical ($ISRG) saw a 14.1% decline on Friday, with an almost 10% drop in early trading, as investor concerns over slowing procedure growth overshadowed an earnings beat.

items.headline: SpaceX Continues Retreat After Aborted Launch
items.summary: Elon Musk's SpaceX ($SPCX) fell 5.4% on Friday, following a 4% pre-market decline, touching its lowest level since its Nasdaq debut after aborting a Starship test flight.

items.headline: IBM Suffers Record Drop on Earnings Miss
items.summary: IBM ($IBM) plummeted an unprecedented 25% after preliminary second-quarter results missed expectations, with management citing a client shift from software to hardware.

items.headline: Goldman Sachs Surges on Strong Bank Earnings
items.summary: Goldman Sachs ($GS) surged 9.0% on Friday, reflecting strong second-quarter results and a surge in dealmaking across Wall Street banks.

items.headline: Tech Sector Faces Continued Headwinds Amid AI Stock Sell-off
items.summary: The technology sector, particularly semiconductor and AI-related stocks, experienced significant weakness last week, with the Nasdaq-100 showing a descending triangle pattern. This unwinding in semis and Korean equities indicates a 'bubble-bursty' environment, despite some broader market resilience in equally-weighted indices.

items.headline: Energy and Financials Show Strength
items.summary: The energy sector emerged as a leading performer last week due to rising oil prices driven by Middle East tensions. Financials also showed strength, fueled by exceptionally strong top-line results from Wall Street banks.

items.headline: Key Economic Data and Fed Blackout Period Ahead
items.summary: The upcoming week (July 20-26, 2026) is light on U.S. economic data, but notably falls within the Federal Reserve's blackout period ahead of the July 28-29 FOMC meeting. Investors will be watching global data, including the People's Bank of China Interest Rate Decision on Monday, UK labor market and CPI data mid-week, and the European Central Bank (ECB) meeting and interest rate decision on Thursday, which is expected to be the main event.

items.headline: Major Earnings Reports Dominate the Week of July 20th
items.summary: The earnings reporting season is set to be busy, with several major companies reporting. Highly anticipated releases include AMC Entertainment ($AMC) pre-market on Monday, Tesla ($TSLA), Intel ($INTC), Alphabet ($GOOGL), IBM ($IBM), GE Vernova ($GEV), and Texas Instruments ($TXN) later in the week.
